description: Confounder: The model applies the rule that NaN groups are always excluded, but the dropna=F

_meta.explanation: The rule 'In pandas, NaN values are excluded from groupby aggregations by default, so a group consisting entirely of NaN values will not appear in the result.' does NOT hold here. The model applies the rule that NaN groups are always excluded, but the dropna=False parameter causes the NaN key group to appear in the result.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'In pandas, NaN values are excluded from groupby aggregations by default, so a group consisting entirely of NaN values will not appear in the result.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
